controls.description: Injection via historian and SCADA data feeds
Assess injection risk at every agent data ingestion point; Assess agent permission scope as part of OT risk register; Assess agent memory stores as OT data integrity risk
Input validation mandatory at OT data boundary
Excessive privilege is specifically listed as OT vulnerability class; Injection via OT data feeds is a documented attack vector

Least privilege must be enforced at zone boundary for all automated systems; Mandatory: safety function must be independent of AI decision layer; GenAI guardrails must be independent of model inference layer
Memory corruption and state manipulation; Lateral movement between control systems; Safety system bypass is the highest severity OT threat
Authenticate all automated system-to-system communications; Circuit breakers between OT automation layers; Input validation mandatory at OT data boundary
Third-party tool components in OT; Expanded to include agentic AI components; Third-party OT tool data leakage
Tool integration approval process; SBOM and vendor assessment for agentic stack; Tool data scope in OT security assessment
Vendor assessment for OT tool providers; Formal vendor programme for OT agent components; Vendor programme for OT data suppliers
OT data exfiltration via compromised automation
Monitor all outbound data from OT zone; Monitor for unexpected exfiltration of OT training data; Monitor outputs for OT data disclosure patterns
Governance policy for autonomous OT systems; Compliance programme for OT GenAI deployments; Data lineage as OT governance requirement
Cascading failure across OT components; OT GenAI pipeline failure as availability risk
OT data confidentiality requirements apply to training data; OT data in retrieval corpora requires access control; OT knowledge encoded in models requires protection
OT data must not be disclosed without authorisation; Inference inputs are OT data and require protection
Corpus manipulation directly threatens OT operational integrity

controls.description: Prompt injection documented in OT risk assessment for each LLM integration

controls.title: Control
controls.description: Application

controls.title: SI-10
controls.description: Validate all inputs to LLMs connected to OT systems � reject inputs containing injection indicators; Training data validation � adversarial content detected and rejected before training; LLM outputs validated before passing to OT systems � schema validation, allowlist enforcement

controls.title: SI-3
controls.description: Treat prompt injection as a malicious code analog � detection and response controls required; LLM output scanning for malicious content before OT system ingestion; Analogy: LLM misinformation detection controls as an integrity assurance layer on advisory outputs

controls.title: AC-3
controls.description: LLM access to OT systems enforced by policy � injection cannot escalate LLM access beyond defined scope; LLM access to OT data enforced by classification � sensitive process and network data requires elevated access tier; LLM access to OT systems enforced by policy � scope cannot be exceeded regardless of model instruction

controls.title: Section 5.4 � Threats
controls.description: LLMs with historian access as a new vector for automated OT intelligence gathering

controls.title: Section 6.2 � Risk assessment
controls.description: OT data classification applied to all data accessible by LLMs

controls.control_id: Protecting OT data at rest and in transit
controls.title: Section 7.3 � Data protection
controls.description: Encryption and access controls on all OT data paths feeding LLM context

controls.title: SC-28
controls.description: OT data used in LLM context encrypted at rest � historian exports, embedding stores, prompt caches; System prompts encrypted at rest � not stored in cleartext configuration files; OT vector store content encrypted at rest

controls.title: AU-9
controls.description: LLM access logs to OT data protected � audit trail of all OT data accessed by LLM; System prompt access logs protected � unauthorised access attempts detectable

controls.description: ML SBOM and component integrity verification as supply chain controls

controls.description: LLM vendors subject to same supply chain security requirements as OT software vendors

controls.description: Security requirements applied to all LLM component vendors � provenance, integrity, vulnerability disclosure

controls.title: SR-3
controls.description: Documented supply chain security plan covering LLM components in OT deployment

controls.title: SR-6
controls.description: Periodic security assessment of LLM vendors with OT-deployed components

controls.description: Model poisoning scenarios included in OT risk assessment for each LLM; Vector store integrity included in OT LLM risk assessment

controls.description: Independent validation of LLM outputs against rule-based reference systems

controls.title: SI-7
controls.description: Model integrity verification before each OT deployment � hash-based integrity check; Vector store integrity monitoring � alert on anomalous content or unexpected modifications

controls.title: AU-12
controls.description: Full audit trail of LLM outputs � poisoning indicators detectable through output analysis; All LLM actions in OT context logged � full accountability for every OT data access and any recommended action; LLM resource consumption logged � patterns indicating exhaustion attacks detectable
